data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [OVERLAY]. Target: the sibling Phase-1 plan "Author 3 eval specs + offline discipline tests (Phase 1)" (completion-verifier / ticket-quality / spec-alignment specs). Recorded here for traceability (the eval-suite family is owned in this ticket's neighborhood; this plan relates to the shipped seam). Tool-grounded against src/rebar/llm/eval.py and src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/. Disposition: CLEAN / NOT-APPLICABLE — no findings.

T1 is an overlay that engages only when a bright line is crossed (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architecture, performance/scalability target, migration). None fires for THIS plan — it is additive authoring of three packaged eval specs ON an already-shipped internal framework, plus offline tests — so per T1's anti-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires") the chunk PASSES.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 dimensions this plan satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):

- T1(a) prior art considered / not reinventing: The plan does NOT build a new eval architecture or a new registry — it explicitly mirrors "the plan-review/code-quality pattern, using the schema + registry from the foundation task." Tool-verified the prior art it reuses is shipped: src/rebar/llm/eval.py is the Inspect-AI eval seam (deterministic-scorer-gates / llm-judge-reports-only, pinned cross-family grader, at_least(k) over epochs, coverage_threshold, frozen gold_set + Cohen's-kappa); the registry/typo-gate mechanism it leans on already exists (validate_scorer's `known` frozenset rejects any deterministic name not in rebar.llm.eval_scorers.REGISTRY — eval.py:107-132); and the established specs it parallels ship under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (plan-review-finder/verifier/isf-finder, code-quality). This is reuse of a well-trodden in-house pattern, not reinvention.

- T1(a) anti-repackaging (Rule-of-Three): the registry the new specs name is DELEGATED to a named "foundation task," not re-derived here — so this child does not stand up a third bespoke registry shape; the prior-art consideration is explicitly deferred to the owning task. No duplicate-architecture risk for T1 to flag in this plan.

- T1(b) novelty justification — NOT APPLICABLE / well-trodden. No novel architectural pattern is introduced. The three case-type/dataset-tagging conventions, the deterministic-gating-scorer + report-only-cross-family-judge discipline, the at_least(k)/coverage/gold_set knobs, and the offline-validity unit tests are all conventional eval-harness practice layered onto the existing seam — nothing to justify against an established approach.

- T1(c) unverified capability assertions — RESOLVED. The load-bearing capability claims are verifiable from the repo, not asserted: (i) "each spec validate_eval_spec-clean" + "scorers registered" + "judge disciplined + gates:false" are exactly what the shipped validator enforces (validate_scorer rejects gates:true judges and requires a pinned cross-family grader; the `known`-registry gate rejects unregistered/typo'd scorer names — eval.py:107-135); (ii) the generator model id `anthropic:claude-opus-4-8` matches the convention already carried by the shipped specs (code-quality.eval.yaml:8, plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:11), so it is not a novel/unverified "library supports X" assertion; (iii) the GPT-4o cross-family report-only judge with temp 0 / seed / dated snapshot / gates:false is precisely the discipline the seam already implements. No open capability gap requiring a SPIKE.

No findings for T1. Clean for this pass.
